seo_description: Current time in Ahmedabad, India. India Standard Time (IST, UTC+5:30) year-round — no DST. Live clock and time differences for Ahmedabad.

faq.question: What time is it in Ahmedabad right now?
faq.answer: Ahmedabad, Gujarat, India uses India Standard Time (IST, UTC+5:30) year-round. India does not observe Daylight Saving Time. The IANA identifier is Asia/Kolkata (all of India uses a single time zone). Ahmedabad is India's fifth-largest city and a major financial and industrial center. The live clock above shows the current Ahmedabad time.

faq.question: What time zone is Ahmedabad in?
faq.answer: Ahmedabad, India uses India Standard Time (IST, UTC+5:30) year-round. The IANA identifier is Asia/Kolkata. All of India — from Mumbai to Kolkata, Delhi to Chennai, and Ahmedabad — uses the same single time zone (IST, UTC+5:30) without Daylight Saving Time. India's unique half-hour offset (+5:30 rather than +5 or +6) applies to all cities.

faq.question: Does Ahmedabad observe daylight saving time?
faq.answer: No. Ahmedabad, India does not observe Daylight Saving Time. India uses IST (UTC+5:30) permanently year-round. India has considered introducing DST but has never implemented it — the country spans a wide longitude but uses a single time zone.

faq.question: What is the time difference between Ahmedabad and London?
faq.answer: Ahmedabad (IST, UTC+5:30) is 5 hours and 30 minutes ahead of London (GMT, UTC+0) in winter. During UK summer time (BST, UTC+1), Ahmedabad is 4 hours and 30 minutes ahead. India does not observe DST.

faq.question: What is the time difference between Ahmedabad and New York?
faq.answer: Ahmedabad (IST, UTC+5:30) is 10 hours and 30 minutes ahead of New York (EST, UTC-5) in winter. During US DST (EDT, UTC-4), Ahmedabad is 9 hours and 30 minutes ahead. India does not observe DST.

faq.question: What are typical business hours in Ahmedabad?
faq.answer: Standard business hours in Ahmedabad are 10:00 AM to 7:00 PM IST, Monday through Saturday (with Sunday as a rest day for many businesses). Ahmedabad is Gujarat's largest city and one of India's main textile, pharmaceutical, and diamond processing centers. It is home to the Indian Textile Institute and hosts major companies in the chemicals and pharma sectors (Zydus Cadila, Sun Pharmaceutical, Torrent Pharmaceuticals). The Bombay Stock Exchange (BSE) and National Stock Exchange (NSE) are the primary markets for Ahmedabad-listed companies.
